steps.title: Identify what would refute the concern
steps.content: The administrators claim that concerns about virtual tours discouraging in-person visits are “unjustified.” To support this claim, we need evidence showing that virtual tours do NOT actually prevent people from visiting in person — ideally evidence that virtual tours do something other than what the concern predicts.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice C states that many surveyed visitors said the virtual tours convinced them to plan an in-person visit. This directly contradicts the concern: rather than making in-person visits seem unnecessary, the tours actively encouraged visits. The other choices either describe people unaware of the tours (A), demographic info unrelated to tour impact (B), or post-visit behavior that doesn't address the initial decision (D).

That matches choice C — C is correct.
